name: Guinea-Bissau

colors.symbolism: the blood shed by those who died in the struggle for independence
the harvest and the country's wealth
the country's lush vegetation and hope for the future
the African people and continent (the central star)

description: A red vertical band at the hoist, two-fifths of the flag's width, charged with a centred black five-pointed star; at the fly, two equal horizontal stripes — yellow over green — taking the remaining three-fifths.
history: Adopted on 24 September 1973 when the African Party for the Independence of Guinea and Cape Verde (PAIGC) unilaterally declared the country's independence from Portugal; this was recognised by Portugal on 10 September 1974. The design is based on the flag of the PAIGC, with the same Pan-African red-yellow-green palette and Black Star of Africa used by Cape Verde's first independence flag (1975–1992) and by the related flag of Ghana.

features.symbolism: a red vertical band at the hoist taking the inner two-fifths, with two equal horizontal stripes (yellow over green) on the outer three-fifths; the Pan-African colours are arranged as in the related flags of Cape Verde (1975–1992) and Guinea, reflecting the PAIGC liberation movement
African unity and the people of the continent, in the tradition of the Pan-African Black Star
